step.text: Submit MOA, rules, member list, address proof
step.name: Step 4

mainEntity.name: Society vs Section 8 Company — which is better?

acceptedAnswer.text: Society: simpler compliance, state-level registration, minimum 7 members. Section 8 Company: central (MCA) registration, stronger corporate governance, better for large-scale operations and foreign funding. Section 8 is preferred for FCRA registration.

mainEntity.name: Can a society receive tax exemption?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Apply for 12A (income exemption) and 80G (donor deduction) under the Income Tax Act. Both available for registered societies with charitable objects.

Association of 7+ persons established for charitable, literary, scientific, or social welfare purposes. Governed by Societies Registration Act, 1860. State-level registration with Registrar of Societies.
Minimum 7 members for registration. No upper limit. Members can be individuals or institutions. Managing committee of minimum 7 members governs the society.

Yes, but income must be applied towards objects of the society. Cannot distribute surplus/profit to members. Surplus reinvested in charitable activities. Can earn from events, publications, services within objects.

mainEntity.name: Is society eligible for tax exemption?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with registration under Section 12A/12AB of IT Act. Donors get Section 80G deduction. Society must apply 85% of income towards objects annually to maintain exemption.

mainEntity.name: Society vs Trust vs Section 8 — which to choose?

acceptedAnswer.text: Society: for membership-based organisations (clubs, associations). Trust: for asset-holding charitable purposes. Section 8: for institutional credibility (FCRA preferred, higher compliance). Choose based on purpose and donor expectations.
